[client-error-handling] [P1] Export error components from shared/ui barrel
tasks.description:
Add the three new error fallback components to the `layers/shared/ui/index.ts` barrel export file.

## File to Modify

**`apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/index.ts`**

Add these three export lines at the end of the file (before the closing of exports, or in a logical grouping with other standalone components like `ConnectionStatusBanner` and `FeatureDisabledState`):

```typescript
export { RouteErrorFallback } from './route-error-fallback';
export { NotFoundFallback } from './not-found-fallback';
export { AppCrashFallback } from './app-crash-fallback';
```

Place them after the existing `FeatureDisabledState` export (line 173 in the current file) to keep the error-related components grouped with other standalone UI components.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `RouteErrorFallback`, `NotFoundFallback`, and `AppCrashFallback` are all importable via `import { ... } from '@/layers/shared/ui'`
- No other exports are removed or reordered
- The barrel file follows the existing pattern (named exports from relative paths)
tasks.activeForm: Exporting error components from shared/ui barrel

tasks.subject: [client-error-handling] [P2] Add react-error-boundary dependency to client package
tasks.description:
Add the `react-error-boundary` package as a dependency of the client app. This package provides the `ErrorBoundary` component used to wrap the `<Root />` component in `main.tsx`, and the `FallbackProps` type used by `AppCrashFallback`.

## Command to Run

From the repo root:
```bash
cd apps/client && pnpm add react-error-boundary
```

Or from the monorepo root:
```bash
pnpm --filter @dorkos/client add react-error-boundary
```

## Verification

After installation, verify:
1. `apps/client/package.json` contains `"react-error-boundary"` in `dependencies`
2. `pnpm-lock.yaml` is updated
3. The package provides the `ErrorBoundary` component and `FallbackProps` type

## Acceptance Criteria

- `react-error-boundary` is listed in `apps/client/package.json` dependencies (latest version)
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` is updated with the resolved version
- `import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'` works in client code
- `import type { FallbackProps } from 'react-error-boundary'` works in client code

tasks.subject: [client-error-handling] [P3] Create error-states-sections.ts for Dev Playground
tasks.description:
Create the section registry file that defines the searchable/navigable sections for the Error States playground page.

## File to Create

**`apps/client/src/dev/sections/error-states-sections.ts`**

```typescript
import type { PlaygroundSection } from '../playground-registry';

/** Error states sections for the dev playground error states page. */
export const ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS: PlaygroundSection[] = [
  {
    id: 'route-error-fallback',
    title: 'Route Error Fallback',
    page: 'error-states',
    category: 'Error States',
    keywords: ['error', 'boundary', 'crash', 'route', 'fallback', 'retry'],
  },
  {
    id: 'not-found-fallback',
    title: 'Not Found Fallback',
    page: 'error-states',
    category: 'Error States',
    keywords: ['404', 'not found', 'missing', 'page'],
  },
  {
    id: 'app-crash-fallback',
    title: 'App Crash Fallback',
    page: 'error-states',
    category: 'Error States',
    keywords: ['crash', 'fatal', 'reload', 'catastrophic', 'inline styles'],
  },
  {
    id: 'error-toasts',
    title: 'Error Toasts',
    page: 'error-states',
    category: 'Error States',
    keywords: ['toast', 'notification', 'mutation', 'query', 'sonner'],
  },
];
```

Note: The `page` field uses `'error-states'` which must be added to the `Page` type union in `playground-registry.ts` (done in task 3.4).

## Acceptance Criteria

- File exports `ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS` as a `PlaygroundSection[]`
- Four sections defined: route-error-fallback, not-found-fallback, app-crash-fallback, error-toasts
- All sections have `page: 'error-states'` and `category: 'Error States'`
- Keywords are relevant for Cmd+K search discovery
- Follows the exact pattern of existing section files (e.g., `forms-sections.ts`, `topology-sections.ts`)

tasks.subject: [client-error-handling] [P3] Create ErrorStateShowcases.tsx for Dev Playground
tasks.description:
Create the showcase component that demonstrates all error state components in the Dev Playground. ## File to Create **`apps/client/src/dev/showcases/ErrorStateShowcases.tsx`** This file uses the `PlaygroundSection` + `ShowcaseDemo` + `ShowcaseLabel` pattern established by other showcases (e.g., `FormShowcases.tsx`). ```typescript import { useState } from 'react'; import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'; import { toast } from 'sonner'; import { PlaygroundSection } from '../PlaygroundSection'; import { ShowcaseLabel } from '../ShowcaseLabel'; import { ShowcaseDemo } from '../ShowcaseDemo'; import { RouteErrorFallback } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { NotFoundFallback } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { AppCrashFallback } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; /** * Controlled component that throws when `shouldThrow` is true. * Used with ErrorBoundary + resetKeys to demonstrate error/recovery cycle. */ function ErrorTrigger({ shouldThrow }: { shouldThrow: boolean }) { if (shouldThrow) throw new Error('Controlled showcase error'); return

No error — component is healthy.

; } /** Error state component showcases: RouteErrorFallback, NotFoundFallback, AppCrashFallback, toasts. */ export function ErrorStateShowcases() { const [shouldThrow, setShouldThrow] = useState(false); return ( <> Interactive — Toggle Error
( setShouldThrow(false)} info={{ componentStack: '' }} /> )} resetKeys={[shouldThrow]} >
Static Preview
Static Preview
{}} />

This component uses inline styles only. If context providers crash, any dependency on them would also crash — so this fallback has zero bundle dependencies.

Trigger Toasts

Chat-specific error states (TransportErrorBanner, ErrorMessageBlock) are showcased on the Chat page.

); } ``` ### Key Implementation Notes - The `RouteErrorFallback` showcase uses `ErrorBoundary` with `resetKeys={[shouldThrow]}` so toggling the state resets the boundary automatically. - The `RouteErrorFallback` expects `ErrorComponentProps` which includes `error`, `reset`, and `info`. In the showcase, we wrap it inside an `ErrorBoundary` FallbackComponent to provide a realistic demo. - The `AppCrashFallback` showcase renders it statically with a mock error. The `resetErrorBoundary` prop is provided as a no-op since it is not used by the component (it only calls `window.location.reload()`). - The `AppCrashFallback` has its own dark background via inline styles, so it renders as a self-contained dark panel inside the showcase. - Error toasts use `sonner`'s `toast.error()` directly, matching how they are called in the `QueryCache` and `MutationCache` handlers. ## Acceptance Criteria - Four showcase sections rendered: Route Error Fallback, Not Found Fallback, App Crash Fallback, Error Toasts - Route Error Fallback has an interactive toggle that triggers/resets an error boundary - Not Found Fallback renders statically in a bordered container - App Crash Fallback renders statically with explanatory note about inline-styles constraint - Error Toasts section has buttons that trigger different toast configurations - Cross-reference note points to the Chat page for chat-specific error states - Follows the `PlaygroundSection` + `ShowcaseDemo` + `ShowcaseLabel` pattern

tasks.subject: [client-error-handling] [P3] Create ErrorStatesPage.tsx for Dev Playground
[client-error-handling] [P3] Wire error-states into playground-config, playground-registry, and DevPlayground
tasks.description:
Update three existing dev playground files to register the new Error States page.

## Files to Modify

### 1. `apps/client/src/dev/playground-registry.ts`

**Add `'error-states'` to the `Page` type union** (currently lines 1-13):

Current:
```typescript
export type Page =
  | 'overview'
  | 'tokens'
  | 'forms'
  | 'components'
  | 'chat'
  | 'features'
  | 'promos'
  | 'command-palette'
  | 'simulator'
  | 'topology'
  | 'filter-bar';
```

New:
```typescript
export type Page =
  | 'overview'
  | 'tokens'
  | 'forms'
  | 'components'
  | 'chat'
  | 'features'
  | 'promos'
  | 'command-palette'
  | 'simulator'
  | 'topology'
  | 'filter-bar'
  | 'error-states';
```

**Add the export** for `ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS` (after the existing section exports, around line 38):
```typescript
export { ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS } from './sections/error-states-sections';
```

**Add the import alias** (after the existing import aliases, around line 50):
```typescript
import { ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS as errorStates } from './sections/error-states-sections';
```

**Spread into `PLAYGROUND_REGISTRY`** (after `...filterBar`, around line 68):
```typescript
export const PLAYGROUND_REGISTRY: PlaygroundSection[] = [
  ...tokens,
  ...forms,
  ...components,
  ...chat,
  ...features,
  ...promos,
  ...commandPalette,
  ...simulator,
  ...topology,
  ...filterBar,
  ...errorStates,
];
```

### 2. `apps/client/src/dev/playground-config.ts`

**Add the import** for `AlertTriangle` from lucide-react (add to existing import at top, line 1-12):
```typescript
import {
  Palette,
  TextCursorInput,
  Component,
  MessageSquare,
  Blocks,
  Play,
  Megaphone,
  Terminal,
  Network,
  Filter,
  AlertTriangle,
} from 'lucide-react';
```

**Add the import** for `ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS` (add to existing imports from `./playground-registry`, line 13-25):
```typescript
import {
  TOKENS_SECTIONS,
  FORMS_SECTIONS,
  COMPONENTS_SECTIONS,
  CHAT_SECTIONS,
  FEATURES_SECTIONS,
  PROMOS_SECTIONS,
  COMMAND_PALETTE_SECTIONS,
  SIMULATOR_SECTIONS,
  TOPOLOGY_SECTIONS,
  FILTER_BAR_SECTIONS,
  ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS,
} from './playground-registry';
```

**Add the PageConfig entry** to `PAGE_CONFIGS[]`. Place it in the "Design System" group after "Components" (since error states are design-system-level components, not feature-specific):

```typescript
{
  id: 'error-states',
  label: 'Error States',
  description: 'Error boundaries, 404 pages, crash fallbacks, and toast notifications.',
  icon: AlertTriangle,
  group: 'design-system',
  sections: ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS,
  path: 'error-states',
},
```

### 3. `apps/client/src/dev/DevPlayground.tsx`

**Add the import** for `ErrorStatesPage` (after existing page imports, around line 38):
```typescript
import { ErrorStatesPage } from './pages/ErrorStatesPage';
```

**Add to `PAGE_COMPONENTS` map** (after the existing entries, around line 80):
```typescript
const PAGE_COMPONENTS: Record<string, React.ComponentType<PlaygroundPageProps>> = {
  overview: OverviewPage as React.ComponentType<PlaygroundPageProps>,
  tokens: TokensPage,
  forms: FormsPage,
  components: ComponentsPage,
  chat: ChatPage,
  features: FeaturesPage,
  topology: TopologyPage,
  promos: PromosPage,
  'command-palette': CommandPalettePage,
  simulator: SimulatorPage,
  'error-states': ErrorStatesPage,
};
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Page` type includes `'error-states'`
- `ERROR_STATES_SECTIONS` is exported from `playground-registry.ts` and spread into `PLAYGROUND_REGISTRY`
- `PAGE_CONFIGS` has a new entry for `error-states` in the `design-system` group with `AlertTriangle` icon
- `PAGE_COMPONENTS` maps `'error-states'` to `ErrorStatesPage`
- Navigating to `/dev/error-states` in the browser renders the Error States page
- The Error States page appears in the sidebar under "Design System"
- Cmd+K search finds error-related sections by keywords
- No existing page configurations are modified or removed
